column1: ## NOTES – Disagreements with current config.py values
## Field format: crop | variable | current → sourced | severity | explanation
## Vanilla | t_ideal_lo | 20→21 | MINOR | FAO post-harvest compendium explicitly states 21°C as lower ideal bound
column2: 20°C is within tolerance not the productive ideal.

column1: ## Vanilla | t_tol_hi | 33→35 | MINOR | ECOCROP tol_hi is 35°C
column2: 33°C is too conservative.

column1: ## Vanilla | shade_ideal | [50,60]→[30,50] | SIGNIFICANT | Current ideal band sits at the high-shade end. Commercial vanilla agroforestry practice (CIRAD/ICRAF) targets 30–50% shade for productive vines
column2: 50–60% is used only in exposed open sites. Keeping shade_ideal at [50,60] will cause the optimizer to over-shade vanilla unnecessarily and clash with coconut wide spacing which delivers ~35–45%.

column1: ## Vanilla | shade_tol_lo | 35→20 | MINOR | 20% shade is survivable (verified from low-shade vanilla trials)
column2: 35% tolerance lower bound is too conservative.

column1: ## Cocoa | t_ideal_hi | 32→28 | SIGNIFICANT | Multiple peer-reviewed sources (Läderach 2013
column2: Springer physiological review 2018) show peak growth and yield at 21–28°C. Temperatures 28–32°C are tolerated but production quality declines. Using 32 as the ideal upper bound will make the suitability scorer too permissive above 28°C.

column1: ## Cocoa | t_tol_lo | 18→15 | MINOR | ECOCROP lower tolerance is 15°C
column2: 18°C is the lower practical threshold for India but not the true ECOCROP tol_lo.

column1: ## Cocoa | shade_ideal_lo | 25→30 | MINOR | Cabruca agroforestry review (Springer 2022) and PFAF confirm that below 25% shade young cocoa suffers
column2: 30% is the safe practical floor.

column1: ## Black pepper | t_tol_lo | 18→10 | SIGNIFICANT | TNAU explicitly states "tolerates 10–40°C
column2: below [10°C] pollination and fruit set adversely affected." The current tol_lo of 18°C is the threshold for optimal zone (Pattukkottai never drops that low anyway), but the ECOCROP/TNAU tol_lo is 10°C. Mismatched tol_lo makes the suitability scorer wrong for transferred macroclimates.

column1: ## Black pepper | t_tol_hi | 35→40 | SIGNIFICANT | Same TNAU source: tol_hi 40°C. Current 35 would penalise pepper in hot Pattukkottai summers (~38–40°C ambient) more than the literature warrants.
## Black pepper | rh_ideal_lo | 70→60 | MINOR | TNAU says >60–70% for vine development
column2: setting ideal_lo at 70 is slightly stricter than the source.

column1: ## Nutmeg | shade_ideal | [40,50]→[25,50] | SIGNIFICANT | Current ideal band [40,50] is too narrow and too high for mature intercropped nutmeg. KSSDB and Kerala agroforestry practice use 25–50% for established trees. The optimizer will over-constrain nutmeg placement.
## Nutmeg | shade_tol | [20,72]→[10,65] | MINOR | tol_lo 10% verified from mature nutmeg in mixed orchards
column2: tol_hi 65% (not 72%) consistent with sources.

column1: ## Ginger | t_ideal_lo | 20→22 | MINOR | ICAR/IISR: productive range starts 22–25°C
column2: 20°C is suboptimal (rhizome yield reduced).

column1: ## Ginger | t_tol_lo | 16→13 | MINOR | ICAR-CCARI: below 13°C dormancy induced
column2: 16°C overstates cold sensitivity.

column1: ## Ginger | rh_ideal_hi | 90→80 | MODERATE | Agrownet/IISR: optimal RH 70–80%
column2: 90% is the upper tolerance not the ideal. Scoring ginger as "ideal" above 80% will inflate suitability in over-humid conditions where disease risk is high.

column1: ## Pomegranate | shade_ideal_hi | 20→10 | MODERATE | Pomegranate is a full-sun semi-arid fruit. >15% shade measurably reduces fruit set and Brix (agriculture.institute source). The current ideal_hi of 20% is too permissive.
## Pomegranate | shade_tol_hi | 45→35 | MODERATE | Above 35% shade the tree becomes unproductive
column2: current 45 over-extends tolerance.

column1: ## Pomegranate | rh_ideal_hi | 85→65 | SIGNIFICANT | This is the most important correction. Pomegranate thrives in semi-arid conditions (RH 40–65%). The current ideal_hi of 85% places pomegranate in the "ideal" zone during Pattukkottai's NE monsoon (Oct–Dec, RH ~85–90%) when in reality that window is exactly when bacterial blight and fruit-cracking risk spikes. Setting ideal_hi at 65 correctly links the growth envelope to the disease risk – high RH is not ideal even if the tree survives it.
## Pomegranate | rh_tol_hi | 95→90 | MINOR | Consistent tightening.
## Guava | t_ideal_hi | 30→28 | MINOR | Agriculture.Institute and NHB: quality production peaks at 23–28°C
column2: 30°C is within tolerance but not ideal.

column1: ## Guava | t_tol_hi | 38→42 | MINOR | NHB states guava tolerates up to 42°C.
## Guava | shade_ideal_hi | 25→15 | MODERATE | Guava is a full-sun fruit
column2: >15% shade suppresses flowering and fruit development.

column1: ## Guava | rh_ideal_hi | 85→70 | MODERATE | High RH with heavy rain consistently degrades fruit quality and promotes anthracnose (agriculture.institute). Ideal upper bound 70% not 85%.
## Mango | t_ideal_hi | 30→27 | MODERATE | TNAU Mango PDF (2026, direct primary source): "ideal temperature range 24–27°C." Current 30 is too permissive.
## Mango | shade_ideal_hi | 20→10 | MODERATE | TNAU and ICAR PPQS: mango is a full-sun crop
column2: shade >10% at orchard scale is counterproductive.

column1: ## Mango | rh_ideal_hi | 80→75 | MINOR | TNAU: "moderate humidity during flowering"
column2: high humidity during flowering detrimental. Lowering ideal_hi reflects this.

column1: ## Grapes | rh_ideal_hi | 70→65 | MINOR | TNAU Horticulture 2020 and MapMyCrop: "dry humid condition preferred
column2: cloudy/high RH/fog not suitable." 65% better reflects the boundary where powdery mildew risk rises sharply (disease model threshold rh_sat=85% but growth ideal should not extend there).

column1: ## Grapes | rh_tol_hi | 88→80 | MODERATE | Above 80% RH both downy and powdery mildew reach near-saturating infection pressure in the disease layer (rh_sat=85). Keeping tol_hi at 88 while the disease model fires at 85 creates an inconsistency: the growth score stays high while disease is already suppressing viability.
## Banana | t_ideal_lo | 25→22 | MODERATE | NHB: grow well range 15–35°C
column2: mean optimal ~27°C. The current ideal_lo of 25 incorrectly penalises banana in cool early mornings (20–24°C ambient is common outside the Pattukkottai hot season). 22°C is the practical productive lower bound.

column1: ## Banana | t_ideal_hi | 35→32 | MODERATE | Above 32°C banana shows heat stress (delayed bunch emergence, smaller fingers). NHB mean optimal is 27°C
column2: 32°C is the upper productive threshold, 38°C is the physiological survival limit.

column1: ## Banana | rh_ideal_lo | 60→75 | SIGNIFICANT | NHB explicitly states "RH 75–85%" as the requirement. The current ideal_lo of 60% would score banana as "ideal" in quite dry conditions where it actually suffers from water stress
column2: this is the most actionable correction for the suitability scorer on banana.

column1: ## Banana | wind_ideal_max | 2.0→3.0 | MODERATE | NHB: strong wind >4 m/s causes pseudostem lodging. The current ideal_max of 2.0 m/s is too restrictive
column2: 3.0 m/s is a realistic upper-ideal for a sheltered site.

column1: ## Banana | wind_tol_max | 4.0→5.0 | MINOR | NHB warns >4 m/s
column2: 5.0 m/s is the practical storm threshold before lodging is near-certain.

column1: ## Dragon fruit | t_ideal_hi | 33→29 | MODERATE | PictureThisAI/Wikifarmer (Hylocereus undatus): optimal 20–29°C
column2: above 29°C photosynthesis decreases and sunburn risk rises. 33°C is within tolerance not ideal.

column1: ## Dragon fruit | shade_ideal_lo | 10→20 | MODERATE | Frontiers Plant Science (2025): sunburn eliminated only at ≥25% shade net
column2: H. undatus (dominant Indian variety) shows best yield at 20–40% shade. The current ideal_lo of 10% is too permissive for the sunburn-prevention objective.
